title: Downhole valve apparatus
abstract: A downhole valve apparatus for closing or restricting flow in a wellbore, the downhole valve apparatus comprising a valve member having an open position and a closed position, the valve member rotatable around a rotation axis of the valve member between the open and closed positions; an interface member comprising a drive recess; and a valve operator cooperable with the interface member to provide rotation of the valve member around the rotation axis between the open and closed positions, the valve operator comprising a valve member engaging portion rotatably mounted with the valve member and a drive recess engaging portion slidably mounted with the drive recess. The drive recess engaging portion and the drive recess comprise complimentary engagable surfaces to provide a robust interface between the interface member and the valve member and prevent stress concentrations occurring between the respective components.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS: 1. A downhole valve apparatus comprising: a valve member having an open position and a closed position, the valve member rotatable around a rotation axis of the valve member between the open and closed positions; an interface member comprising a drive recess; and a valve operator cooperable with the interface member to provide rotation of the valve member around the rotation axis between the open and closed positions, the valve operator comprising a valve member engaging portion rotatably mounted with the valve member and a drive recess engaging portion slidably mounted with the drive recess; wherein the drive recess engaging portion and the drive recess comprise complimentary engagable surfaces. 2. The downhole valve apparatus of claim 1, wherein the complimentary engagable surfaces comprise an end surface of the drive recess and an operation surface of the drive recess engaging portion of the valve operator, wherein the end surface and the operation surface are cooperable to provide rotation of the valve member around the rotation axis between the open and closed positions. 3. The downhole valve apparatus of claim 2, wherein the end surface and the operation surface maintain sliding contact during rotation of the valve member around the rotation axis between the open and closed positions. 4. The downhole valve apparatus of any preceding claim, wherein the valve member comprises a hole, and wherein the valve member engaging portion of the valve operator is rotatably mounted within the hole of the valve member. 5. The downhole valve apparatus of claim 4, wherein an inner surface of the hole and an outer surface of the valve member engaging portion of the valve operator are correspondingly shaped to maintain rotatable contact across substantially the entire area of the inner and outer surfaces. 6. The downhole valve apparatus of claim 4 or 5, wherein the hole is eccentrically located with respect to the rotation axis. 7. The downhole valve apparatus of any preceding claim, wherein the valve member and the interface member are configured to move relative to each other in a lost motion movement phase without rotation of the valve member. 8. The downhole valve apparatus of claim 7, wherein the drive recess engaging portion of the valve operator is axially slidable within the drive recess in the lost motion movement phase 9. The downhole valve apparatus of claim 7 or 8, wherein the complimentary engagable surfaces comprise a longitudinal surface of the drive recess engaging portion of the valve operator and an elongate surface of the drive recess. 10. The downhole valve apparatus of claim 9, wherein the longitudinal surface of the drive recess engaging portion of the valve operator and the elongate surface of the drive recess maintain sliding contact in the lost motion movement phase and temporarily lose contact during rotation of the valve member from the closed position to the open position. 11. The downhole valve apparatus of any of claims 7 to 10, when dependent on claim 2, wherein the end surface of the drive recess and the operation surface of the drive recess engaging portion of the valve operator are not in contact during the lost motion movement phase. 12. The downhole valve of any preceding claim, wherein the valve member comprises a limit stop and the interface member comprises a stop recess, wherein the limit stop is engagable with the stop recess to support the valve member at a predetermined extent of rotation. 13. The downhole valve of claim 12, wherein the stop recess comprises a support surface and the limit stop comprises a stop surface, wherein the stop surface is engagable with the support surface to support the valve member at a predetermined extent of rotation. 

 19 14. The downhole valve of claim 13, wherein the support surface and the stop surface have complementary engaging profiles. 15. The downhole valve member of claim 12, 13 or 14, wherein the stop recess has an axial length greater than an axial length of the drive recess, and wherein the stop recess and the drive recess share a line of symmetry. 16. The downhole valve member of any of claims 12 to 15, when dependent on claim 2, wherein the stop recess is positioned axially relative to the drive recess to permit the stop surface to engage the support surface when the end surface of the drive recess and the operating surface of the drive recess engaging portion of the valve operator are in contact and the valve member has rotated the predetermined extent. 17. The downhole valve apparatus of any of claims 12 to 16, wherein the valve member is a ball valve comprising a truncated face, wherein the limit stop is shaped with a portion of the truncated face. 18. The downhole valve apparatus of claim 17, when dependent on claim 4, wherein the truncated face comprises the hole for rotatably receiving the valve member engaging portion of the valve operator, and wherein the hole is located in an opposing portion of the truncated face to the limit stop. 19. The downhole valve member of claim 17 or 18, wherein the portion of the truncated face is a quadrant of the truncated face. 20. The downhole valve member of any preceding claim, wherein the valve member is formed of a first material and the valve operator is formed of a second material, wherein the first material is different to the second material.
